mappings.rationaleAI: Geographic distribution of life addresses terrestrial (A), aquatic ecosystems (B), organism distribution (C), and biogeographic regions (D).
Ecological principles address the land environment (A.1), life-forms (A.2.c), terrestrial productivity (A.3), and aquatic ecosystems (B).
Forest ecosystems address coniferous, temperate, and tropical biomes (A.1.c), life-forms (A.2), and productivity (A.3).
Savanna ecosystems address grassland/savanna biome (A.1.c), life-forms (A.2), and productivity (A.3).
Desert environments address the desert biome (A.1.c), limiting factors (A.1.b), and adapted life-forms (A.2).
Marine diversity addresses aquatic environment (B.1), oceanic communities (B.2.b), productivity (B.2.d), boundary ecosystems (B.4), and aquatic productivity (B.5).
Ocean science addresses aquatic environment (B.1) and sea as biological environment (B.2.a).
Lake science addresses lacustrine communities (B.3.a) and freshwater productivity (B.5).
Arctic ecology addresses specialized polar biomes (A.1.d).
Antarctic environment addresses specialized polar biomes (A.1.d).
Mountain environments address limiting factors (A.1.b) and habitat-classified life-forms (A.2).

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts

mappings.rationaleAI: Climate dynamics address limiting factors to living on land (A.1.b).
Invasive species address disruption of organism distribution (C).
Glacial-interglacial ecosystem responses address distribution shifts (C) and biogeographic changes (D).
Plant diversity addresses terrestrial life-forms (A.2) and distribution (C).
